content.text:
1	# Task Prompt
2	
3	A ProctorWorks Offline Operations Platform capable of running entirely on a single on-prem machine provides backend APIs for admins to manage a catalog-driven practice product business while also managing reservable study spaces as a physical resource. The platform exposes Authentication and Account APIs supporting local username + password login only, with configurable roles including Platform Admin (full access), Store Admin (manages catalog, promotions, and orders for an assigned store), Content Reviewer (moderation and question bank governance), and Auditor (read-only access to logs and dashboards). Resource Modeling APIs support a study room → zone → seat hierarchy with seat attributes (power outlet, quiet zone, ADA-accessible) and operational statuses (available, disabled, maintenance); seat-map configuration must be versioned, with every publish creating an immutable version record and a required change note of 20–500 characters. Seat reservation operations must enforce inventory locking: a hold expires after 15 minutes if not confirmed, and maintenance seats cannot be held. Commerce Operations APIs cover end-to-end product and inventory: SPU/SKU, categories, brands, specification attributes, tiered/member pricing, batch and expiration date tracking, publish/unpublish with reviewer approval, low-stock alerts at a configurable threshold (default 10 units), and stock-count adjustments requiring a reason code. Marketing Rule APIs support coupons (claim, distribute, redeem, expire), threshold and percentage discounts, first-order offers, campaign time windows, and per-campaign redemption caps; conflict handling must be deterministic using priority (1–1000) then "best customer value" tie-breaker, and a single order may apply at most one coupon plus one automatic promotion. Practice Assessment APIs provide question type management (objective/subjective), bulk import/export, random or rule-based paper generation, auto-grading for objective questions, wrong-answer aggregation, explanation/answer version management, and practice history with redo; a redo must regenerate a new attempt while preserving prior attempts for audit and analytics. The backend uses NestJS to structure domain-focused API modules and TypeORM with PostgreSQL for durable local persistence and transactional integrity; the system must remain fully functional with no internet connectivity and no dependencies on external APIs, third-party OAuth, email/SMS, maps, payments, cloud storage, or push services. Core data models include Users (unique username, password hash, role enum, status), Stores (optional for multi-tenant isolation), Products (SPU) and SKUs (unique per SPU, price in cents, member price tiers), InventoryLots (batch code, expiration date), Orders (state machine with idempotency key required on create), Promotions/Coupons (code uniqueness, validity window, remaining quantity), SeatMapVersions (version number, createdBy, changeNote), Seats (attributes booleans, status enum), Reservations (holdUntil timestamp), Questions/Explanations (versioned), Attempts (score, gradedAt). Validation and observability requirements include configurable data quality rules (completeness, range, uniqueness) producing a 0–100 quality score per dataset, freshness monitoring with a default 24-hour staleness threshold, and local alerting via persisted notifications for admins. Security requires salted password hashing, field-level encryption for sensitive notes, masking in audit exports, row-level access controls by role/store, and immutable audit trails for admin actions retained for 7 years. Performance targets on a single Docker-hosted deployment are p95 ≤ 300 ms for typical reads and ≤ 800 ms for writes at 50 requests/second, with structured logs, trace IDs, and retry-safe idempotent operations for order creation and inventory adjustments.
4

content.text:
1	# Questions and Clarifications — ProctorWorks
2	
3	---
4	
5	## 1. Store Admin Scope — Can They See Other Stores?
6	
7	**Question:** The prompt says Store Admin manages catalog, promotions, and orders for an assigned store. Does this mean they are hard-scoped to one store and cannot see any data from other stores?
8	
9	**Assumption:** Yes — Store Admin is strictly scoped to their assigned store. All queries for products, orders, promotions, and inventory are filtered by `store_id`. Cross-store access returns 403.
10	
11	**Solution:** Every repository query for store-scoped resources checks `store_id` against the authenticated user's assigned store. Platform Admin can see all stores.
12	
13	---
14	
15	## 2. Seat Map Versioning — What Triggers a New Version?
16	
17	**Question:** The prompt says every publish creates an immutable version record. Does editing a seat map without publishing create a draft, or does every save create a version?
18	
19	**Assumption:** Edits are saved as a mutable draft. Publishing the draft creates an immutable `SeatMapVersion` record with a required change note (20–500 chars). The current live version is the latest published version.
20	
21	**Solution:** `seat_map_versions` table stores published snapshots. A `seat_map_draft` table (or draft flag) holds unpublished changes. `POST /seat-maps/:id/publish` validates the change note and creates the immutable version.
22	
23	---
24	
25	## 3. Seat Reservation Hold — What Happens on Expiry?
26	
27	**Question:** A hold expires after 15 minutes if not confirmed. Does the system automatically release expired holds, or does the next request trigger cleanup?
28	
29	**Assumption:** A background job runs every minute to release expired holds (set `holdUntil < now()` → status back to available). Additionally, the hold endpoint checks for expiry before creating a new hold.
30	
31	**Solution:** Scheduled job in NestJS using `@nestjs/schedule` runs every 60 seconds to release expired holds. Hold creation also checks for existing expired holds on the same seat.
32	
33	---
34	
35	## 4. Promotion Conflict — "Best Customer Value" Tie-Breaker
36	
37	**Question:** When two promotions have the same priority, the tie-breaker is "best customer value." How is this calculated — highest discount amount, highest discount percentage, or something else?
38	
39	**Assumption:** Best customer value = the promotion that results in the lowest final order total for the customer. Calculate the discount amount for each tied promotion against the order total and apply the one with the higher discount amount.
40	
41	**Solution:** Promotion engine calculates effective discount for each tied promotion, selects the one with the highest discount amount. If still tied (same discount amount), use the lower promotion ID as a deterministic final tie-breaker.
42	
43	---
44	
45	## 5. Order State Machine — What Are the States?
46	
47	**Question:** The prompt mentions an order state machine but doesn't define the states.
48	
49	**Assumption:** States: `pending` → `confirmed` → `fulfilled` → `cancelled`. Cancellation is only allowed from `pending` or `confirmed`. Refunds are handled as a separate record, not a state.
50	
51	**Solution:** `orders.status` enum: pending, confirmed, fulfilled, cancelled. State transitions enforced in the service layer with explicit allowed-transition checks.
52	
53	---
54	
55	## 6. Idempotency Key — Scope and Behavior
56	
57	**Question:** The prompt requires idempotency keys on order creation and inventory adjustments. What is the scope — per user, per store, or global?
58	
59	**Assumption:** Idempotency keys are globally unique per operation type. A duplicate key on order creation returns the original order response (200, not 201) without creating a new order.
60	
61	**Solution:** `idempotency_keys` table stores `(key, operation_type, response_body, created_at)`. On duplicate key: return stored response. Keys expire after 24 hours.
62	
63	---
64	
65	## 7. Audit Trail Retention — 7 Years
66	
67	**Question:** The prompt requires audit trails retained for 7 years. Does this mean the DB rows must never be deleted, or can they be archived to a separate table/file?
68	
69	**Assumption:** Audit log rows are never deleted from the primary `audit_logs` table. The application DB role has no DELETE permission on `audit_logs`. After 7 years, rows may be archived to a cold storage table but the primary table retains them.
70	
71	**Solution:** PostgreSQL role for the app has INSERT-only on `audit_logs`. A separate archival job (optional) can move rows older than 7 years to `audit_logs_archive`.
72	
73	---
74	
75	## 8. Data Quality Score — What Datasets?
76	
77	**Question:** The prompt says configurable data quality rules produce a 0–100 quality score per dataset. What counts as a dataset — a table, a product, an order?
78	
79	**Assumption:** A dataset is a logical entity type: Products, Orders, Questions, Users, Inventory. Each entity type has configurable rules (completeness, range, uniqueness). The quality score is computed on demand or on a schedule for each entity type.
80	
81	**Solution:** `data_quality_rules` table stores rules per entity type. `data_quality_scores` table stores the latest computed score per entity type with timestamp. A scheduled job recomputes scores every hour.
82	
83	---
84	
85	## 9. Field-Level Encryption — Which Fields?
86	
87	**Question:** The prompt says sensitive notes require field-level encryption. Which fields exactly?
88	
89	**Assumption:** Fields to encrypt: `users.notes` (if any), `orders.internal_notes`, `audit_logs.detail` (for sensitive operations). Encryption uses AES-256-GCM with key from env config.
90	
91	**Decision needed:** Confirm the exact list of fields requiring encryption.
92	
93	---
94	
95	## 10. Bulk Import/Export for Questions — What Format?
96	
97	**Question:** The prompt requires bulk import/export for questions. What file format?
98	
99	**Assumption:** CSV for export (simple, evaluator-friendly). JSON for import (supports nested structure for options/answers). Both endpoints are synchronous for reasonable batch sizes (up to 1000 questions).
100	
101	**Solution:** `POST /questions/import` accepts JSON body with array of questions. `GET /questions/export` returns CSV. Both require Content Reviewer or Platform Admin role.
102	
103	---
104	
105	## 11. Wrong-Answer Aggregation — What Does It Aggregate?
106	
107	**Question:** The prompt mentions wrong-answer aggregation. What is the output — a report per question showing how many times each wrong answer was chosen?
108	
109	**Assumption:** For each objective question, track how many times each incorrect option was selected across all attempts. Expose as `GET /questions/:id/wrong-answer-stats` returning option → count mapping.
110	
111	**Solution:** `attempt_answers` table stores per-question per-attempt selected option. Aggregation query groups by question_id and selected_option where correct=false.
112	
113	---
114	
115	## 12. Practice History Redo — Does It Copy the Same Questions?
116	
117	**Question:** A redo must regenerate a new attempt while preserving prior attempts. Does redo use the same question set as the original, or regenerate using the same rules?
118	
119	**Assumption:** Redo regenerates using the same paper generation rules (same paper_id), not the same question instances. This means a redo may get different random questions if the paper uses random selection. Prior attempts are preserved and linked to the same paper.
120	
121	**Solution:** `attempts` table has `paper_id` and `parent_attempt_id` (nullable). Redo creates a new attempt with the same `paper_id` and sets `parent_attempt_id` to the original attempt ID.
122	
123	---
124	
125	## 13. Low-Stock Alert — How Is It Delivered?
126	
127	**Question:** The prompt says low-stock alerts at a configurable threshold. How are alerts delivered — email, in-app notification, or both?
128	
129	**Assumption:** Alerts are persisted as notifications in the `notifications` table (local alerting only, no email/SMS per the offline constraint). Platform Admin and Store Admin can query their notifications via API.
130	
131	**Solution:** `notifications` table stores `(user_id, type, message, read, created_at)`. A background job checks inventory levels every 15 minutes and creates notifications for items below threshold.
132	
133	---
134	
135	## 14. Freshness Monitoring — What Is Monitored?
136	
137	**Question:** The prompt says freshness monitoring with a 24-hour staleness threshold. What data is monitored for freshness?
138	
139	**Assumption:** Monitored datasets: Products (last updated), Inventory (last stock adjustment), Questions (last reviewed). If any dataset hasn't been updated in 24 hours, a staleness notification is created for admins.
140	
141	**Solution:** `data_freshness_checks` table stores last-checked timestamp per entity type. Scheduled job runs every hour and creates notifications for stale datasets.
142	
143	---
144	
145	## 15. Performance — p95 ≤ 300ms — How Is This Enforced?
146	
147	**Question:** The prompt specifies p95 ≤ 300ms for reads. Is this a hard requirement that must be tested, or a design target?
148	
149	**Assumption:** Design target enforced through proper indexing, query optimization, and connection pooling. Not tested with load tests in the submission, but documented in the README with the indexing strategy.
150	
151	**Solution:** Add database indexes on all foreign keys and frequently queried columns. Use TypeORM query builder for complex queries to avoid N+1. Document index strategy in `docs/design.md`.
152

content.text:
1	# ProctorWorks — Feature Overview
2	
3	Offline-first backend API platform built with NestJS + TypeORM + PostgreSQL. No UI. Pure backend.
4	
5	---
6	
7	## Authentication and Accounts
8	
9	What it does: Local username/password login with role-based access.
10	
11	What needs to be built:
12	- Login endpoint with bcrypt password verification
13	- JWT token issuance (local, no external IdP)
14	- Role enum: platform_admin, store_admin, content_reviewer, auditor
15	- Store assignment for store_admin role
16	- Account lockout after failed attempts
17	- Password change endpoint
18	- Audit log on login/logout/role change
19	
20	---
21	
22	## Resource Modeling — Study Rooms, Zones, Seats
23	
24	What it does: Hierarchical physical space management with versioned seat maps.
25	
26	What needs to be built:
27	- CRUD for study rooms, zones, seats
28	- Seat attributes: power_outlet, quiet_zone, ada_accessible (booleans)
29	- Seat status enum: available, disabled, maintenance
30	- Seat map versioning: draft → publish creates immutable SeatMapVersion
31	- Change note validation: 20–500 characters required on publish
32	- Seat map version history endpoint
33	
34	---
35	
36	## Seat Reservations
37	
38	What it does: Atomic seat holds with 15-minute expiry.
39	
40	What needs to be built:
41	- Hold endpoint: creates reservation with holdUntil = now + 15 min
42	- Maintenance seats cannot be held (400 error)
43	- Confirm endpoint: transitions hold to confirmed
44	- Cancel endpoint: releases hold
45	- Background job: releases expired holds every 60 seconds
46	- Conflict check: seat already held returns 409
47	
48	---
49	
50	## Commerce — Products (SPU/SKU)
51	
52	What it does: Full product catalog with categories, brands, pricing tiers.
53	
54	What needs to be built:
55	- SPU (Standard Product Unit) CRUD
56	- SKU CRUD (unique per SPU, price in cents)
57	- Categories and brands CRUD
58	- Specification attributes (key-value pairs per SKU)
59	- Tiered/member pricing per SKU
60	- Publish/unpublish with Content Reviewer approval workflow
61	- Low-stock alert at configurable threshold (default 10 units)
62	
63	---
64	
65	## Commerce — Inventory
66	
67	What it does: Batch and expiration date tracking with reason-coded adjustments.
68	
69	What needs to be built:
70	- InventoryLot CRUD (batch code, expiration date, quantity)
71	- Stock adjustment endpoint (requires reason code)
72	- Idempotency key on adjustments
73	- Low-stock notification trigger
74	- Expiration date alerts
75	
76	---
77	
78	## Commerce — Orders
79	
80	What it does: End-to-end order lifecycle with state machine.
81	
82	What needs to be built:
83	- Order create with idempotency key (required)
84	- State machine: pending → confirmed → fulfilled → cancelled
85	- Order line items linked to SKUs
86	- Promotion/coupon application at order time
87	- Order history per store
88	
89	---
90	
91	## Marketing Rules — Promotions and Coupons
92	
93	What it does: Discount engine with deterministic conflict resolution.
94	
95	What needs to be built:
96	- Coupon CRUD (code, validity window, remaining quantity)
97	- Claim, distribute, redeem, expire endpoints
98	- Threshold discounts (order total > X → discount Y)
99	- Percentage discounts
100	- First-order offers
101	- Campaign time windows and per-campaign redemption caps
102	- Conflict resolution: priority (1–1000) then best customer value
103	- One coupon + one automatic promotion max per order
104	
105	---
106	
107	## Practice Assessment — Questions
108	
109	What it does: Question bank with versioned explanations and bulk operations.
110	
111	What needs to be built:
112	- Question CRUD (objective/subjective types)
113	- Answer options for objective questions
114	- Explanation/answer versioning
115	- Bulk import (JSON) and export (CSV)
116	- Wrong-answer aggregation stats per question
117	- Content Reviewer approval workflow
118	
119	---
120	
121	## Practice Assessment — Papers and Attempts
122	
123	What it does: Paper generation, auto-grading, and practice history.
124	
125	What needs to be built:
126	- Paper generation: random or rule-based question selection
127	- Attempt create (starts a practice session)
128	- Submit attempt with answers
129	- Auto-grading for objective questions
130	- Score calculation and gradedAt timestamp
131	- Practice history per user
132	- Redo: new attempt from same paper, preserves prior attempts
133	- Wrong-answer aggregation across attempts
134	
135	---
136	
137	## Data Quality and Observability
138	
139	What it does: Configurable quality scoring and freshness monitoring.
140	
141	What needs to be built:
142	- Data quality rules CRUD (completeness, range, uniqueness per entity type)
143	- Quality score computation (0–100) per dataset
144	- Freshness monitoring (24-hour staleness threshold)
145	- Persisted notifications for admins
146	- Scheduled jobs for quality and freshness checks
147	
148	---
149	
150	## Security
151	
152	What it does: Encryption, masking, row-level access, immutable audit trail.
153	
154	What needs to be built:
155	- bcrypt password hashing
156	- AES-256-GCM field-level encryption for sensitive notes
157	- Masking in audit log exports
158	- Row-level access control by role and store_id
159	- Append-only audit_logs table (no DELETE for app role)
160	- 7-year retention policy (no delete, optional archive)
161	- Trace IDs on every request
162

100	│   │   ├── notifications.controller.ts
101	│   │   └── entities/
102	│   └── audit/
103	│       ├── audit.module.ts
104	│       ├── audit.service.ts
105	│       └── entities/audit-log.entity.ts
106	├── unit_tests/
107	│   ├── auth.spec.ts
108	│   ├── reservations.spec.ts
109	│   ├── orders.spec.ts
110	│   ├── promotions.spec.ts
111	│   └── assessments.spec.ts
112	├── API_tests/
113	│   ├── auth.api.spec.ts
114	│   ├── rooms.api.spec.ts
115	│   ├── reservations.api.spec.ts
116	│   ├── products.api.spec.ts
117	│   ├── inventory.api.spec.ts
118	│   ├── orders.api.spec.ts
119	│   ├── promotions.api.spec.ts
120	│   ├── questions.api.spec.ts
121	│   └── assessments.api.spec.ts
122	├── migrations/                       # TypeORM migration files
123	├── run_tests.sh
124	├── docker-compose.yml
125	├── Dockerfile
126	├── .env.example
127	├── package.json
128	├── tsconfig.json
129	├── nest-cli.json
130	└── README.md
131	```
132	
133	---
134	
135	## Submission Checklist
136	
137	- [ ] `docker compose up` completes without errors
138	- [ ] Cold start tested in clean environment
139	- [ ] README has startup command, ports, test credentials
140	- [ ] `docs/design.md` and `docs/api-spec.md` present
141	- [ ] `unit_tests/` and `API_tests/` exist, `run_tests.sh` passes
142	- [ ] No `node_modules/`, `dist/`, or compiled output in ZIP
143	- [ ] No real credentials in any config file
144	- [ ] All prompt requirements implemented
145	- [ ] `sessions/develop-1.json` trajectory file present
146	- [ ] `metadata.json` at root with all required fields
147	- [ ] Swagger UI at `http://localhost:3000/api/docs`
148

content.text:
1	# ProctorWorks — Design Document
2	
3	## 1. Overview
4	
5	ProctorWorks is an offline-first backend API platform. No UI. Pure REST API built with NestJS + TypeORM + PostgreSQL. Runs on a single Docker host with no external connectivity.
6	
7	---
8	
9	## 2. Architecture
10	
11	```
12	HTTP Client (Postman / frontend)
13	  │
14	  ▼
15	NestJS HTTP Server (port 3000)
16	  ├── Global Exception Filter     → structured JSON errors
17	  ├── Trace ID Interceptor        → X-Trace-Id on every request
18	  ├── JWT Auth Guard              → validates Bearer token
19	  ├── Roles Guard                 → RBAC enforcement
20	  ├── Validation Pipe             → class-validator on all DTOs
21	  └── Domain Controllers/Services
22	        │
23	        ▼
24	   TypeORM Repository layer
25	        │
26	        ▼
27	   PostgreSQL (port 5432)
28	```
29	
30	---
31	
32	## 3. Technology Stack
33	
34	| Layer | Choice |
35	|---|---|
36	| HTTP framework | NestJS |
37	| ORM | TypeORM |
38	| Database | PostgreSQL 16 |
39	| Auth | JWT (local, no external IdP) |
40	| Password hashing | bcrypt (rounds=12) |
41	| Field encryption | AES-256-GCM |
42	| Validation | class-validator + class-transformer |
43	| Scheduling | @nestjs/schedule |
44	| API docs | @nestjs/swagger (Swagger UI at /api/docs) |
45	| Logging | Winston with structured JSON |
46	| Container | Docker + docker-compose |
47	
48	---
49	
50	## 4. Module Responsibilities
51	
52	| Module | Responsibility |
53	|---|---|
54	| `auth` | Login, JWT, password hashing, account management |
55	| `rooms` | Study rooms, zones, seats, seat map versioning |
56	| `reservations` | Seat holds, confirm, cancel, expiry job |
57	| `products` | SPU, SKU, categories, brands, spec attributes, pricing |
58	| `inventory` | InventoryLots, stock adjustments, low-stock alerts |
59	| `orders` | Order lifecycle, state machine, idempotency |
60	| `promotions` | Coupons, discounts, campaigns, conflict resolution |
61	| `questions` | Question bank, versioned explanations, bulk ops |
62	| `assessments` | Papers, attempts, grading, history, redo |
63	| `quality` | Data quality rules, scores, freshness monitoring |
64	| `notifications` | Persisted admin notifications |
65	| `audit` | Append-only audit log, 7-year retention |
66	| `common` | Guards, interceptors, filters, decorators, pipes |
67	
68	---
69	
70	## 5. Data Model
71	
72	### Auth
73	
74	```
75	users
76	  id uuid PK
77	  username text UNIQUE NOT NULL
78	  password_hash text NOT NULL          -- bcrypt
79	  role enum NOT NULL                   -- platform_admin | store_admin | content_reviewer | auditor
80	  store_id uuid FK stores (nullable)   -- only for store_admin
81	  status enum DEFAULT active           -- active | suspended | locked
82	  failed_login_count int DEFAULT 0
83	  locked_until timestamptz
84	  created_at timestamptz
85	  updated_at timestamptz
86	
87	stores
88	  id uuid PK
89	  name text UNIQUE NOT NULL
90	  created_at timestamptz
91	```
92	
93	### Rooms and Seats
94	
95	```
96	study_rooms
97	  id uuid PK
98	  name text NOT NULL
99	  created_at timestamptz
100	
101	zones
102	  id uuid PK
103	  room_id uuid FK study_rooms
104	  name text NOT NULL
105	
106	seats
107	  id uuid PK
108	  zone_id uuid FK zones
109	  label text NOT NULL
110	  power_outlet bool DEFAULT false
111	  quiet_zone bool DEFAULT false
112	  ada_accessible bool DEFAULT false
113	  status enum DEFAULT available        -- available | disabled | maintenance
114	
115	seat_map_versions
116	  id uuid PK
117	  room_id uuid FK study_rooms
118	  version_number int NOT NULL
119	  created_by uuid FK users
120	  change_note text NOT NULL            -- 20-500 chars
121	  snapshot jsonb NOT NULL              -- full seat map at publish time
122	  created_at timestamptz NOT NULL
123	  UNIQUE (room_id, version_number)
124	
125	reservations
126	  id uuid PK
127	  seat_id uuid FK seats
128	  user_id uuid FK users
129	  status enum DEFAULT hold             -- hold | confirmed | cancelled | expired
130	  hold_until timestamptz NOT NULL
131	  confirmed_at timestamptz
132	  cancelled_at timestamptz
133	  created_at timestamptz
134	```
135	
136	### Commerce
137	
138	```
139	categories
140	  id uuid PK
141	  name text UNIQUE NOT NULL
142	  parent_id uuid FK categories (nullable)
143	
144	brands
145	  id uuid PK
146	  name text UNIQUE NOT NULL
147	
148	products (SPU)
149	  id uuid PK
150	  store_id uuid FK stores
151	  name text NOT NULL
152	  category_id uuid FK categories
153	  brand_id uuid FK brands
154	  status enum DEFAULT draft            -- draft | pending_review | published | unpublished
155	  created_at timestamptz
156	
157	skus
158	  id uuid PK
159	  product_id uuid FK products
160	  sku_code text UNIQUE NOT NULL
161	  price_cents int NOT NULL
162	  member_price_cents int
163	  attributes jsonb                     -- spec attributes
164	  created_at timestamptz
165	
166	sku_price_tiers
167	  id uuid PK
168	  sku_id uuid FK skus
169	  tier_name text NOT NULL
170	  price_cents int NOT NULL
171	
172	inventory_lots
173	  id uuid PK
174	  sku_id uuid FK skus
175	  batch_code text NOT NULL
176	  expiration_date date
177	  quantity int NOT NULL DEFAULT 0
178	  created_at timestamptz
179	
180	inventory_adjustments
181	  id uuid PK
182	  lot_id uuid FK inventory_lots
183	  delta int NOT NULL                   -- positive = add, negative = remove
184	  reason_code text NOT NULL
185	  idempotency_key text UNIQUE NOT NULL
186	  adjusted_by uuid FK users
187	  created_at timestamptz
188	
189	orders
190	  id uuid PK
191	  store_id uuid FK stores
192	  user_id uuid FK users
193	  status enum DEFAULT pending          -- pending | confirmed | fulfilled | cancelled
194	  idempotency_key text UNIQUE NOT NULL
195	  total_cents int NOT NULL
196	  discount_cents int DEFAULT 0
197	  coupon_id uuid FK coupons (nullable)
198	  promotion_id uuid FK promotions (nullable)
199	  created_at timestamptz
200	  updated_at timestamptz
201	
202	order_items
203	  id uuid PK
204	  order_id uuid FK orders
205	  sku_id uuid FK skus
206	  quantity int NOT NULL
207	  unit_price_cents int NOT NULL
208	```
209	
210	### Promotions
211	
212	```
213	promotions
214	  id uuid PK
215	  store_id uuid FK stores
216	  name text NOT NULL
217	  type enum                            -- threshold | percentage | first_order
218	  priority int NOT NULL                -- 1-1000
219	  discount_type enum                   -- fixed_cents | percentage
220	  discount_value int NOT NULL
221	  min_order_cents int                  -- for threshold type
222	  starts_at timestamptz
223	  ends_at timestamptz
224	  redemption_cap int
225	  redemption_count int DEFAULT 0
226	  active bool DEFAULT true
227	
228	coupons
229	  id uuid PK
230	  store_id uuid FK stores
231	  code text UNIQUE NOT NULL
232	  promotion_id uuid FK promotions
233	  remaining_quantity int
234	  starts_at timestamptz
235	  ends_at timestamptz
236	  status enum DEFAULT active           -- active | expired | exhausted
237	
238	coupon_claims
239	  id uuid PK
240	  coupon_id uuid FK coupons
241	  user_id uuid FK users
242	  claimed_at timestamptz
243	  redeemed_at timestamptz
244	  order_id uuid FK orders (nullable)
245	```
246	
247	### Questions and Assessments
248	
249	```
250	questions
251	  id uuid PK
252	  store_id uuid FK stores
253	  type enum NOT NULL                   -- objective | subjective
254	  body text NOT NULL
255	  status enum DEFAULT draft            -- draft | pending_review | approved | rejected
256	  created_by uuid FK users
257	  created_at timestamptz
258	
259	question_options
260	  id uuid PK
261	  question_id uuid FK questions
262	  body text NOT NULL
263	  is_correct bool NOT NULL
264	
265	question_explanations
266	  id uuid PK
267	  question_id uuid FK questions
268	  version_number int NOT NULL
269	  body text NOT NULL
270	  created_by uuid FK users
271	  created_at timestamptz
272	  UNIQUE (question_id, version_number)
273	
274	papers
275	  id uuid PK
276	  store_id uuid FK stores
277	  name text NOT NULL
278	  generation_rule jsonb NOT NULL       -- random count or rule-based filters
279	  created_by uuid FK users
280	  created_at timestamptz
281	
282	paper_questions
283	  paper_id uuid FK papers
284	  question_id uuid FK questions
285	  position int NOT NULL
286	  PRIMARY KEY (paper_id, question_id)
287	
288	attempts
289	  id uuid PK
290	  paper_id uuid FK papers
291	  user_id uuid FK users
292	  parent_attempt_id uuid FK attempts (nullable)  -- for redo
293	  status enum DEFAULT in_progress      -- in_progress | submitted | graded
294	  score decimal
295	  graded_at timestamptz
296	  started_at timestamptz
297	  submitted_at timestamptz
298	
299	attempt_answers
300	  id uuid PK
301	  attempt_id uuid FK attempts
302	  question_id uuid FK questions
303	  selected_option_id uuid FK question_options (nullable)
304	  text_answer text                     -- for subjective
305	  is_correct bool                      -- set on grading
306	```
307	
308	### Quality and Audit
309	
310	```
311	data_quality_rules
312	  id uuid PK
313	  entity_type text NOT NULL            -- products | orders | questions | users | inventory
314	  rule_type enum                       -- completeness | range | uniqueness
315	  config jsonb NOT NULL
316	  active bool DEFAULT true
317	
318	data_quality_scores
319	  id uuid PK
320	  entity_type text NOT NULL
321	  score decimal NOT NULL               -- 0-100
322	  computed_at timestamptz NOT NULL
323	
324	notifications
325	  id uuid PK
326	  user_id uuid FK users
327	  type text NOT NULL
328	  message text NOT NULL
329	  read bool DEFAULT false
330	  created_at timestamptz
331	
332	audit_logs
333	  id uuid PK
334	  actor_id uuid FK users
335	  action text NOT NULL
336	  resource_type text
337	  resource_id uuid
338	  detail jsonb
339	  trace_id text
340	  created_at timestamptz NOT NULL
341	  -- NO UPDATE, NO DELETE for app DB role
342	
343	idempotency_keys
344	  key text PK
345	  operation_type text NOT NULL
346	  response_body jsonb NOT NULL
347	  created_at timestamptz NOT NULL
348	```
349	
350	---
351	
352	## 6. Key Flows
353	
354	### Seat Reservation Hold
355	
356	```
357	1. POST /reservations {seat_id, user_id}
358	2. Check seat.status != maintenance → else 400
359	3. Check no active hold on seat (holdUntil > now) → else 409
360	4. INSERT reservations (status=hold, holdUntil=now+15min)
361	5. Return 201 + reservation
362	
363	Background job (every 60s):
364	UPDATE reservations SET status=expired WHERE status=hold AND holdUntil < now()
365	UPDATE seats SET status=available WHERE id IN (expired reservations)
366	```
367	
368	### Order with Promotion
369	
370	```
371	1. POST /orders {idempotency_key, items, coupon_code?}
372	2. Check idempotency_key not in idempotency_keys → else return stored response
373	3. Calculate subtotal from items
374	4. Find applicable automatic promotions (active, within time window, not capped)
375	5. If coupon_code provided: validate coupon (active, not expired, not exhausted)
376	6. Resolve conflicts: sort by priority desc, tie-break by best customer value
377	7. Apply at most one coupon + one automatic promotion
378	8. INSERT order + order_items
379	9. Store idempotency_key + response
380	10. Return 201
381	```
382	
383	### Promotion Conflict Resolution
384	
385	```
386	1. Collect all eligible promotions (automatic + coupon's linked promotion)
387	2. Sort by priority DESC
388	3. If tie on priority: calculate discount amount for each, pick highest
389	4. If still tied: pick lower UUID as deterministic tie-breaker
390	5. Apply selected promotion(s) — max one coupon + one automatic
391	```
392	
393	---
394	
395	## 7. Security Design
396	
397	- Passwords: bcrypt with 12 rounds
398	- JWT: HS256, secret from env, 8-hour expiry
399	- Field encryption: AES-256-GCM for `orders.internal_notes`, `users.notes`
400	- Audit log: INSERT-only for app DB role, no UPDATE/DELETE
401	- Row-level access: store_admin queries always filtered by `store_id`
402	- Trace IDs: UUID generated per request, attached to all log lines and response header `X-Trace-Id`
403	- Sensitive fields masked in audit export: password_hash, encrypted fields shown as `[REDACTED]`
404	
405	---
406	
407	## 8. Background Jobs
408	
409	| Job | Interval | Description |
410	|---|---|---|
411	| Release expired holds | 60s | Set expired reservations to expired status |
412	| Low-stock check | 15 min | Create notifications for SKUs below threshold |
413	| Expiration date check | 1 hour | Create notifications for lots expiring within 7 days |
414	| Data quality score | 1 hour | Recompute quality scores per entity type |
415	| Freshness check | 1 hour | Create notifications for stale datasets |
416	| Coupon expiry | 1 hour | Set expired coupons to expired status |
417	
418	---
419	
420	## 9. Error Handling
421	
422	All errors return:
423	```json
424	{
425	  "statusCode": 400,
426	  "code": "VALIDATION_ERROR",
427	  "message": "human readable message",
428	  "traceId": "uuid"
429	}
430	```
431	
432	Standard codes: VALIDATION_ERROR (400), UNAUTHORIZED (401), FORBIDDEN (403), NOT_FOUND (404), CONFLICT (409), IDEMPOTENCY_CONFLICT (409), INTERNAL_ERROR (500)
433	
434	---
435	
436	## 10. Docker Setup
437	
438	```yaml
439	services:
440	  api:
441	    build: .
442	    ports:
443	      - "3000:3000"
444	    environment:
445	      DATABASE_URL: postgres://proctorworks:proctorworks@db:5432/proctorworks
446	      JWT_SECRET: ${JWT_SECRET}
447	      ENCRYPTION_KEY: ${ENCRYPTION_KEY}
448	    depends_on:
449	      db:
450	        condition: service_healthy
451	
452	  db:
453	    image: postgres:16
454	    environment:
455	      POSTGRES_USER: proctorworks
456	      POSTGRES_PASSWORD: proctorworks
457	      POSTGRES_DB: proctorworks
458	    healthcheck:
459	      test: ["CMD-SHELL", "pg_isready -U proctorworks"]
460	      interval: 5s
461	      retries: 10
462	    volumes:
463	      - pgdata:/var/lib/postgresql/data
464	
465	volumes:
466	  pgdata:
467	```
468	
469	---
470	
471	## 11. Performance Strategy
472	
473	- Index all foreign keys
474	- Index `reservations.hold_until`, `reservations.status`
475	- Index `orders.idempotency_key`, `inventory_adjustments.idempotency_key`
476	- Index `coupons.code`, `promotions.priority`
477	- Index `audit_logs.created_at`, `audit_logs.actor_id`
478	- TypeORM query builder for complex joins (avoid N+1)
479	- Connection pool: min 2, max 10
480	- Response compression via NestJS compression middleware
481

content.text:
1	# ProctorWorks — Feature Build Order
2	
3	Build one slice at a time. Each slice must be fully working (implementation + tests) before moving to the next.
4	
5	---
6	
7	## Slice 1 — Project Foundation
8	Done when:
9	- NestJS app boots with TypeORM connected to PostgreSQL
10	- `docker compose up` starts cleanly
11	- Migrations run on startup
12	- Structured logging with trace IDs on every request
13	- Health endpoint `GET /health` returns 200
14	- `.env.example` has all required vars
15	- `run_tests.sh` runs unit + API tests
16	
17	---
18	
19	## Slice 2 — Authentication and RBAC
20	Done when:
21	- `POST /auth/login` validates username/password, returns JWT
22	- JWT middleware protects all routes
23	- Role enum: platform_admin, store_admin, content_reviewer, auditor
24	- Store assignment on store_admin accounts
25	- Wrong role → 403
26	- Account lockout after 5 failed attempts (15 min lockout)
27	- Audit log on login, logout, role change
28	- Unit tests: password hashing, JWT validation, role checks, lockout
29	- API tests: login, wrong password, lockout, wrong role 403
30	
31	---
32	
33	## Slice 3 — Resource Modeling (Rooms, Zones, Seats)
34	Done when:
35	- CRUD for study rooms, zones, seats
36	- Seat attributes: power_outlet, quiet_zone, ada_accessible
37	- Seat status: available, disabled, maintenance
38	- Seat map draft → publish flow
39	- Change note validation (20–500 chars) on publish
40	- Immutable SeatMapVersion created on publish
41	- Version history endpoint
42	- API tests: CRUD, publish with invalid note, version history
43	
44	---
45	
46	## Slice 4 — Seat Reservations
47	Done when:
48	- `POST /reservations` creates hold (holdUntil = now + 15 min)
49	- `GET /reservations` lists reservations (filtered by user or seat)
50	- Maintenance seat hold returns 400
51	- Already-held seat returns 409
52	- `POST /reservations/:id/confirm` confirms hold
53	- `POST /reservations/:id/cancel` releases hold
54	- Confirm on expired hold returns 409
55	- Background job releases expired holds every 60 seconds
56	- Unit tests: hold expiry logic, maintenance seat rejection, expired confirm
57	- API tests: hold, confirm, cancel, expired hold confirm 409, maintenance seat 400
58	
59	---
60	
61	## Slice 5 — Commerce: Products and Catalog
62	Done when:
63	- SPU CRUD, SKU CRUD (unique per SPU)
64	- Categories and brands CRUD
65	- Specification attributes per SKU
66	- Tiered/member pricing per SKU
67	- Publish/unpublish with Content Reviewer approval
68	- Store-scoped access for store_admin
69	- API tests: CRUD, publish workflow, cross-store 403
70	
71	---
72	
73	## Slice 6 — Commerce: Inventory
74	Done when:
75	- InventoryLot CRUD (batch code, expiration date, quantity)
76	- Stock adjustment with required reason code
77	- Idempotency key on adjustments
78	- Low-stock notification at configurable threshold (default 10)
79	- Expiration date alert notifications
80	- Unit tests: idempotency, low-stock trigger
81	- API tests: adjustment, duplicate key, low-stock notification
82	
83	---
84	
85	## Slice 7 — Commerce: Orders
86	Done when:
87	- `POST /orders` requires idempotency key
88	- State machine: pending → confirmed → fulfilled → cancelled
89	- `POST /orders/:id/confirm`, `POST /orders/:id/fulfill`, `POST /orders/:id/cancel`
90	- Order line items linked to SKUs
91	- Duplicate idempotency key returns original order (200)
92	- Cancellation only from pending/confirmed → else 409
93	- Store-scoped order history
94	- Unit tests: state machine transitions (all valid + invalid), idempotency
95	- API tests: create, confirm, fulfill, cancel, duplicate key, cancel from fulfilled 409
96	
97	---
98	
99	## Slice 8 — Marketing Rules: Promotions and Coupons
100	Done when:
101	- Coupon CRUD (code, validity window, remaining quantity)
102	- Claim, distribute, redeem endpoints
103	- `POST /coupons/:id/expire` manually expires a coupon
104	- Threshold and percentage discount promotions
105	- First-order offer detection
106	- Campaign time windows and per-campaign redemption caps
107	- Conflict resolution: priority then best customer value
108	- Deterministic tie-breaker: lower UUID if discount amounts equal
109	- One coupon + one automatic promotion max per order
110	- Unit tests: conflict resolution, tie-breaker determinism, cap enforcement
111	- API tests: claim, redeem, expired coupon 409, conflict resolution, cap reached
112	
113	---
114	
115	## Slice 9 — Practice Assessment: Questions
116	Done when:
117	- Question CRUD (objective/subjective)
118	- Answer options for objective questions
119	- Explanation/answer versioning
120	- Bulk import (JSON) and export (CSV)
121	- Wrong-answer aggregation stats per question
122	- Content Reviewer approval workflow
123	- API tests: CRUD, bulk import/export, wrong-answer stats
124	
125	---
126	
127	## Slice 10 — Practice Assessment: Papers and Attempts
128	Done when:
129	- Paper generation: random or rule-based
130	- Attempt create and submit
131	- Auto-grading for objective questions
132	- Score calculation
133	- Practice history per user
134	- Redo: new attempt from same paper, preserves prior attempts
135	- Unit tests: auto-grading, redo preserves history
136	- API tests: full attempt flow, redo, history
137	
138	---
139	
140	## Slice 11 — Data Quality and Observability
141	Done when:
142	- Data quality rules CRUD per entity type
143	- Quality score computation (0–100) on demand and scheduled
144	- Freshness monitoring (24-hour threshold)
145	- Persisted notifications for admins
146	- Scheduled jobs for quality and freshness
147	- API tests: quality score, freshness alert, notifications
148	
149	---
150	
151	## Slice 12 — Security Hardening and Audit Log
152	Done when:
153	- AES-256-GCM field-level encryption for sensitive notes
154	- Masking in audit log exports
155	- Append-only audit_logs (no DELETE for app DB role)
156	- 7-year retention enforced at DB level
157	- Audit log query endpoint (Auditor + Platform Admin)
158	- API tests: audit log read, masking in export, 403 for wrong role
159	
160	---
161	
162	## Slice 13 — Final Polish
163	Done when:
164	- `run_tests.sh` passes all unit + API tests
165	- `docker compose up` cold start works
166	- README has startup command, service addresses, test credentials
167	- No node_modules, dist, or compiled output in repo
168	- No real credentials in any config file
169	- Swagger UI available at `/api/docs`
170	- All p95 read queries have proper indexes
171

content.text:
1	openapi: "3.0.3"
2	info:
3	  title: ProctorWorks API
4	  version: "1.0.0"
5	  description: |
6	    ProctorWorks Offline Operations Platform.
7	    All endpoints require Bearer JWT unless marked public.
8	    Base URL: http://localhost:3000
9	servers:
10	  - url: http://localhost:3000
11	    description: Local
12	
13	components:
14	  securitySchemes:
15	    bearerAuth:
16	      type: http
17	      scheme: bearer
18	      bearerFormat: JWT
19	
20	  schemas:
21	    Error:
22	      type: object
23	      properties:
24	        statusCode:
25	          type: integer
26	        code:
27	          type: string
28	        message:
29	          type: string
30	        traceId:
31	          type: string
32	
33	security:
34	  - bearerAuth: []
35	
36	paths:
37	  /health:
38	    get:
39	      tags: [Health]
40	      summary: Health check
41	      security: []
42	      responses:
43	        "200":
44	          description: OK
45	          content:
46	            application/json:
47	              schema:
48	                type: object
49	                properties:
50	                  status:
51	                    type: string
52	                    example: ok
53	
54	  /auth/login:
55	    post:
56	      tags: [Auth]
57	      summary: Login
58	      security: []
59	      requestBody:
60	        required: true
61	        content:
62	          application/json:
63	            schema:
64	              type: object
65	              required: [username, password]
66	              properties:
67	                username:
68	                  type: string
69	                password:
70	                  type: string
71	      responses:
72	        "200":
73	          description: Login successful
74	          content:
75	            application/json:
76	              schema:
77	                type: object
78	                properties:
79	                  accessToken:
80	                    type: string
81	                  user:
82	                    type: object
83	                    properties:
84	                      id:
85	                        type: string
86	                        format: uuid
87	                      username:
88	                        type: string
89	                      role:
90	                        type: string
91	        "401":
92	          description: Invalid credentials
93	
94	  /auth/me:
95	    get:
96	      tags: [Auth]
97	      summary: Get current user
98	      responses:
99	        "200":
100	          description: Current user
101	
102	  /auth/change-password:
103	    patch:
104	      tags: [Auth]
105	      summary: Change own password
106	      requestBody:
107	        required: true
108	        content:
109	          application/json:
110	            schema:
111	              type: object
112	              required: [currentPassword, newPassword]
113	              properties:
114	                currentPassword:
115	                  type: string
116	                newPassword:
117	                  type: string
118	                  minLength: 8
119	      responses:
120	        "200":
121	          description: Password changed
122	
123	  /users:
124	    get:
125	      tags: [Users]
126	      summary: List users (Platform Admin only)
127	      parameters:
128	        - in: query
129	          name: page
130	          schema:
131	            type: integer
132	            default: 1
133	        - in: query
134	          name: limit
135	          schema:
136	            type: integer
137	            default: 20
138	      responses:
139	        "200":
140	          description: User list
141	    post:
142	      tags: [Users]
143	      summary: Create user (Platform Admin only)
144	      requestBody:
145	        required: true
146	        content:
147	          application/json:
148	            schema:
149	              type: object
150	              required: [username, password, role]
151	              properties:
152	                username:
153	                  type: string
154	                password:
155	                  type: string
156	                role:
157	                  type: string
158	                  enum: [platform_admin, store_admin, content_reviewer, auditor]
159	                storeId:
160	                  type: string
161	                  format: uuid
162	      responses:
163	        "201":
164	          description: User created
165	
166	  /users/{id}:
167	    patch:
168	      tags: [Users]
169	      summary: Update user
170	      parameters:
171	        - in: path
172	          name: id
173	          required: true
174	          schema:
175	            type: string
176	            format: uuid
177	      requestBody:
178	        content:
179	          application/json:
180	            schema:
181	              type: object
182	              properties:
183	                status:
184	                  type: string
185	                  enum: [active, suspended, locked]
186	                role:
187	                  type: string
188	                storeId:
189	                  type: string
190	                  format: uuid
191	      responses:
192	        "200":
193	          description: Updated
194	    delete:
195	      tags: [Users]
196	      summary: Delete user (Platform Admin only)
197	      parameters:
198	        - in: path
199	          name: id
200	          required: true
201	          schema:
202	            type: string
203	            format: uuid
204	      responses:
205	        "204":
206	          description: Deleted
207	
208	  /rooms:
209	    get:
210	      tags: [Rooms]
211	      summary: List study rooms
212	      responses:
213	        "200":
214	          description: Rooms list
215	    post:
216	      tags: [Rooms]
217	      summary: Create study room (Platform Admin)
218	      requestBody:
219	        required: true
220	        content:
221	          application/json:
222	            schema:
223	              type: object
224	              required: [name]
225	              properties:
226	                name:
227	                  type: string
228	      responses:
229	        "201":
230	          description: Created
231	
232	  /rooms/{id}/zones:
233	    get:
234	      tags: [Rooms]
235	      summary: List zones in room
236	      parameters:
237	        - in: path
238	          name: id
239	          required: true
240	          schema:
241	            type: string
242	            format: uuid
243	      responses:
244	        "200":
245	          description: Zones
246	    post:
247	      tags: [Rooms]
248	      summary: Create zone
249	      parameters:
250	        - in: path
251	          name: id
252	          required: true
253	          schema:
254	            type: string
255	            format: uuid
256	      requestBody:
257	        required: true
258	        content:
259	          application/json:
260	            schema:
261	              type: object
262	              required: [name]
263	              properties:
264	                name:
265	                  type: string
266	      responses:
267	        "201":
268	          description: Created
269	
270	  /zones/{id}/seats:
271	    get:
272	      tags: [Rooms]
273	      summary: List seats in zone
274	      parameters:
275	        - in: path
276	          name: id
277	          required: true
278	          schema:
279	            type: string
280	            format: uuid
281	      responses:
282	        "200":
283	          description: Seats
284	    post:
285	      tags: [Rooms]
286	      summary: Create seat
287	      parameters:
288	        - in: path
289	          name: id
290	          required: true
291	          schema:
292	            type: string
293	            format: uuid
294	      requestBody:
295	        required: true
296	        content:
297	          application/json:
298	            schema:
299	              type: object
300	              required: [label]
301	              properties:
302	                label:
303	                  type: string
304	                powerOutlet:
305	                  type: boolean
306	                quietZone:
307	                  type: boolean
308	                adaAccessible:
309	                  type: boolean
310	      responses:
311	        "201":
312	          description: Created
313	
314	  /rooms/{id}/publish:
315	    post:
316	      tags: [Rooms]
317	      summary: Publish seat map version
318	      parameters:
319	        - in: path
320	          name: id
321	          required: true
322	          schema:
323	            type: string
324	            format: uuid
325	      requestBody:
326	        required: true
327	        content:
328	          application/json:
329	            schema:
330	              type: object
331	              required: [changeNote]
332	              properties:
333	                changeNote:
334	                  type: string
335	                  minLength: 20
336	                  maxLength: 500
337	      responses:
338	        "201":
339	          description: Version created
340	        "400":
341	          description: Invalid change note
342	
343	  /rooms/{id}/versions:
344	    get:
345	      tags: [Rooms]
346	      summary: Get seat map version history
347	      parameters:
348	        - in: path
349	          name: id
350	          required: true
351	          schema:
352	            type: string
353	            format: uuid
354	      responses:
355	        "200":
356	          description: Version history
357	
358	  /reservations:
359	    post:
360	      tags: [Reservations]
361	      summary: Create seat hold
362	      requestBody:
363	        required: true
364	        content:
365	          application/json:
366	            schema:
367	              type: object
368	              required: [seatId]
369	              properties:
370	                seatId:
371	                  type: string
372	                  format: uuid
373	      responses:
374	        "201":
375	          description: Hold created
376	        "400":
377	          description: Seat is in maintenance
378	        "409":
379	          description: Seat already held
380	
381	  /reservations/{id}/confirm:
382	    post:
383	      tags: [Reservations]
384	      summary: Confirm hold
385	      parameters:
386	        - in: path
387	          name: id
388	          required: true
389	          schema:
390	            type: string
391	            format: uuid
392	      responses:
393	        "200":
394	          description: Confirmed
395	        "409":
396	          description: Hold expired
397	
398	  /reservations/{id}/cancel:
399	    post:
400	      tags: [Reservations]
401	      summary: Cancel reservation
402	      parameters:
403	        - in: path
404	          name: id
405	          required: true
406	          schema:
407	            type: string
408	            format: uuid
409	      responses:
410	        "200":
411	          description: Cancelled
412	
413	  /products:
414	    get:
415	      tags: [Products]
416	      summary: List products (store-scoped)
417	      parameters:
418	        - in: query
419	          name: status
420	          schema:
421	            type: string
422	            enum: [draft, pending_review, published, unpublished]
423	        - in: query
424	          name: page
425	          schema:
426	            type: integer
427	            default: 1
428	      responses:
429	        "200":
430	          description: Products
431	    post:
432	      tags: [Products]
433	      summary: Create product (SPU)
434	      requestBody:
435	        required: true
436	        content:
437	          application/json:
438	            schema:
439	              type: object
440	              required: [name, categoryId, brandId]
441	              properties:
442	                name:
443	                  type: string
444	                categoryId:
445	                  type: string
446	                  format: uuid
447	                brandId:
448	                  type: string
449	                  format: uuid
450	      responses:
451	        "201":
452	          description: Created
453	
454	  /products/{id}/skus:
455	    get:
456	      tags: [Products]
457	      summary: List SKUs for product
458	      parameters:
459	        - in: path
460	          name: id
461	          required: true
462	          schema:
463	            type: string
464	            format: uuid
465	      responses:
466	        "200":
467	          description: SKUs
468	    post:
469	      tags: [Products]
470	      summary: Create SKU
471	      parameters:
472	        - in: path
473	          name: id
474	          required: true
475	          schema:
476	            type: string
477	            format: uuid
478	      requestBody:
479	        required: true
480	        content:
481	          application/json:
482	            schema:
483	              type: object
484	              required: [skuCode, priceCents]
485	              properties:
486	                skuCode:
487	                  type: string
488	                priceCents:
489	                  type: integer
490	                memberPriceCents:
491	                  type: integer
492	                attributes:
493	                  type: object
494	      responses:
495	        "201":
496	          description: Created
497	
498	  /products/{id}/publish:
499	    post:
500	      tags: [Products]
501	      summary: Submit for review / publish (Content Reviewer approves)
502	      parameters:
503	        - in: path
504	          name: id
505	          required: true
506	          schema:
507	            type: string
508	            format: uuid
509	      responses:
510	        "200":
511	          description: Status updated
512	
513	  /inventory/lots:
514	    get:
515	      tags: [Inventory]
516	      summary: List inventory lots
517	      parameters:
518	        - in: query
519	          name: skuId
520	          schema:
521	            type: string
522	            format: uuid
523	      responses:
524	        "200":
525	          description: Lots
526	    post:
527	      tags: [Inventory]
528	      summary: Create inventory lot
529	      requestBody:
530	        required: true
531	        content:
532	          application/json:
533	            schema:
534	              type: object
535	              required: [skuId, batchCode, quantity]
536	              properties:
537	                skuId:
538	                  type: string
539	                  format: uuid
540	                batchCode:
541	                  type: string
542	                expirationDate:
543	                  type: string
544	                  format: date
545	                quantity:
546	                  type: integer
547	      responses:
548	        "201":
549	          description: Created
550	
551	  /inventory/adjust:
552	    post:
553	      tags: [Inventory]
554	      summary: Adjust stock (requires reason code and idempotency key)
555	      requestBody:
556	        required: true
557	        content:
558	          application/json:
559	            schema:
560	              type: object
561	              required: [lotId, delta, reasonCode, idempotencyKey]
562	              properties:
563	                lotId:
564	                  type: string
565	                  format: uuid
566	                delta:
567	                  type: integer
568	                reasonCode:
569	                  type: string
570	                idempotencyKey:
571	                  type: string
572	      responses:
573	        "201":
574	          description: Adjustment recorded
575	        "409":
576	          description: Duplicate idempotency key
577	
578	  /orders:
579	    get:
580	      tags: [Orders]
581	      summary: List orders (store-scoped)
582	      responses:
583	        "200":
584	          description: Orders
585	    post:
586	      tags: [Orders]
587	      summary: Create order (idempotency key required)
588	      requestBody:
589	        required: true
590	        content:
591	          application/json:
592	            schema:
593	              type: object
594	              required: [idempotencyKey, items]
595	              properties:
596	                idempotencyKey:
597	                  type: string
598	                items:
599	                  type: array
600	                  items:
601	                    type: object
602	                    properties:
603	                      skuId:
604	                        type: string
605	                        format: uuid
606	                      quantity:
607	                        type: integer
608	                couponCode:
609	                  type: string
610	      responses:
611	        "201":
612	          description: Order created
613	        "200":
614	          description: Duplicate idempotency key — returns existing order
615	
616	  /orders/{id}/confirm:
617	    post:
618	      tags: [Orders]
619	      summary: Confirm order
620	      parameters:
621	        - in: path
622	          name: id
623	          required: true
624	          schema:
625	            type: string
626	            format: uuid
627	      responses:
628	        "200":
629	          description: Confirmed
630	
631	  /orders/{id}/cancel:
632	    post:
633	      tags: [Orders]
634	      summary: Cancel order
635	      parameters:
636	        - in: path
637	          name: id
638	          required: true
639	          schema:
640	            type: string
641	            format: uuid
642	      responses:
643	        "200":
644	          description: Cancelled
645	        "409":
646	          description: Cannot cancel in current state
647	
648	  /promotions:
649	    get:
650	      tags: [Promotions]
651	      summary: List promotions
652	      responses:
653	        "200":
654	          description: Promotions
655	    post:
656	      tags: [Promotions]
657	      summary: Create promotion
658	      requestBody:
659	        required: true
660	        content:
661	          application/json:
662	            schema:
663	              type: object
664	              required: [name, type, priority, discountType, discountValue]
665	              properties:
666	                name:
667	                  type: string
668	                type:
669	                  type: string
670	                  enum: [threshold, percentage, first_order]
671	                priority:
672	                  type: integer
673	                  minimum: 1
674	                  maximum: 1000
675	                discountType:
676	                  type: string
677	                  enum: [fixed_cents, percentage]
678	                discountValue:
679	                  type: integer
680	                minOrderCents:
681	                  type: integer
682	                startsAt:
683	                  type: string
684	                  format: date-time
685	                endsAt:
686	                  type: string
687	                  format: date-time
688	                redemptionCap:
689	                  type: integer
690	      responses:
691	        "201":
692	          description: Created
693	
694	  /coupons:
695	    get:
696	      tags: [Promotions]
697	      summary: List coupons
698	      responses:
699	        "200":
700	          description: Coupons
701	    post:
702	      tags: [Promotions]
703	      summary: Create coupon
704	      requestBody:
705	        required: true
706	        content:
707	          application/json:
708	            schema:
709	              type: object
710	              required: [code, promotionId]
711	              properties:
712	                code:
713	                  type: string
714	                promotionId:
715	                  type: string
716	                  format: uuid
717	                remainingQuantity:
718	                  type: integer
719	                startsAt:
720	                  type: string
721	                  format: date-time
722	                endsAt:
723	                  type: string
724	                  format: date-time
725	      responses:
726	        "201":
727	          description: Created
728	
729	  /coupons/{code}/claim:
730	    post:
731	      tags: [Promotions]
732	      summary: Claim coupon
733	      parameters:
734	        - in: path
735	          name: code
736	          required: true
737	          schema:
738	            type: string
739	      responses:
740	        "200":
741	          description: Claimed
742	        "409":
743	          description: Coupon exhausted or expired
744	
745	  /questions:
746	    get:
747	      tags: [Questions]
748	      summary: List questions
749	      parameters:
750	        - in: query
751	          name: type
752	          schema:
753	            type: string
754	            enum: [objective, subjective]
755	        - in: query
756	          name: status
757	          schema:
758	            type: string
759	      responses:
760	        "200":
761	          description: Questions
762	    post:
763	      tags: [Questions]
764	      summary: Create question
765	      requestBody:
766	        required: true
767	        content:
768	          application/json:
769	            schema:
770	              type: object
771	              required: [type, body]
772	              properties:
773	                type:
774	                  type: string
775	                  enum: [objective, subjective]
776	                body:
777	                  type: string
778	                options:
779	                  type: array
780	                  items:
781	                    type: object
782	                    properties:
783	                      body:
784	                        type: string
785	                      isCorrect:
786	                        type: boolean
787	      responses:
788	        "201":
789	          description: Created
790	
791	  /questions/import:
792	    post:
793	      tags: [Questions]
794	      summary: Bulk import questions (JSON)
795	      requestBody:
796	        required: true
797	        content:
798	          application/json:
799	            schema:
800	              type: object
801	              properties:
802	                questions:
803	                  type: array
804	      responses:
805	        "200":
806	          description: Import result
807	
808	  /questions/export:
809	    get:
810	      tags: [Questions]
811	      summary: Bulk export questions (CSV)
812	      responses:
813	        "200":
814	          description: CSV file
815	          content:
816	            text/csv:
817	              schema:
818	                type: string
819	
820	  /questions/{id}/wrong-answer-stats:
821	    get:
822	      tags: [Questions]
823	      summary: Wrong answer aggregation for a question
824	      parameters:
825	        - in: path
826	          name: id
827	          required: true
828	          schema:
829	            type: string
830	            format: uuid
831	      responses:
832	        "200":
833	          description: Wrong answer stats
834	
835	  /papers:
836	    get:
837	      tags: [Assessments]
838	      summary: List papers
839	      responses:
840	        "200":
841	          description: Papers
842	    post:
843	      tags: [Assessments]
844	      summary: Generate paper
845	      requestBody:
846	        required: true
847	        content:
848	          application/json:
849	            schema:
850	              type: object
851	              required: [name, generationRule]
852	              properties:
853	                name:
854	                  type: string
855	                generationRule:
856	                  type: object

860	      responses:

862	          description: Paper created
863	
864	  /attempts:

866	      tags: [Assessments]
867	      summary: Start attempt
868	      requestBody:
869	        required: true
870	        content:
871	          application/json:
872	            schema:
873	              type: object
874	              required: [paperId]
875	              properties:
876	                paperId:
877	                  type: string
878	                  format: uuid
879	      responses:
880	        "201":
881	          description: Attempt started
882

884	    post:
885	      tags: [Assessments]
886	      summary: Submit attempt with answers
887	      parameters:
888	        - in: path
889	          name: id
890	          required: true
891	          schema:
892	            type: string
893	            format: uuid
894	      requestBody:
895	        required: true
896	        content:
897	          application/json:
898	            schema:
899	              type: object
900	              required: [answers]
901	              properties:
902	                answers:
903	                  type: array
904	                  items:
905	                    type: object
906	                    properties:
907	                      questionId:
908	                        type: string
909	                        format: uuid
910	                      selectedOptionId:
911	                        type: string
912	                        format: uuid
913	                      textAnswer:
914	                        type: string
915	      responses:
916	        "200":
917	          description: Graded result
918	
919	  /attempts/{id}/redo:
920	    post:
921	      tags: [Assessments]
922	      summary: Redo attempt (new attempt from same paper, preserves prior)
923	      parameters:
924	        - in: path
925	          name: id
926	          required: true
927	          schema:
928	            type: string
929	            format: uuid
930	      responses:
931	        "201":
932	          description: New attempt created
933	
934	  /attempts/history:

content.text:
1	# ProctorWorks — AI Self Test (Delivery Acceptance / Project Architecture Review)
2	
3	---
4	
5	## Business/Topic Prompt
6	
7	A ProctorWorks Offline Operations Platform capable of running entirely on a single on-prem machine provides backend APIs for admins to manage a catalog-driven practice product business while also managing reservable study spaces as a physical resource. The platform exposes Authentication and Account APIs supporting local username + password login only, with configurable roles including Platform Admin (full access), Store Admin (manages catalog, promotions, and orders for an assigned store), Content Reviewer (moderation and question bank governance), and Auditor (read-only access to logs and dashboards). Resource Modeling APIs support a study room → zone → seat hierarchy with seat attributes (power outlet, quiet zone, ADA-accessible) and operational statuses (available, disabled, maintenance); seat-map configuration must be versioned, with every publish creating an immutable version record and a required change note of 20–500 characters. Seat reservation operations must enforce inventory locking: a hold expires after 15 minutes if not confirmed, and maintenance seats cannot be held. Commerce Operations APIs cover end-to-end product and inventory: SPU/SKU, categories, brands, specification attributes, tiered/member pricing, batch and expiration date tracking, publish/unpublish with reviewer approval, low-stock alerts at a configurable threshold (default 10 units), and stock-count adjustments requiring a reason code. Marketing Rule APIs support coupons (claim, distribute, redeem, expire), threshold and percentage discounts, first-order offers, campaign time windows, and per-campaign redemption caps; conflict handling must be deterministic using priority (1–1000) then "best customer value" tie-breaker, and a single order may apply at most one coupon plus one automatic promotion. Practice Assessment APIs provide question type management (objective/subjective), bulk import/export, random or rule-based paper generation, auto-grading for objective questions, wrong-answer aggregation, explanation/answer version management, and practice history with redo; a redo must regenerate a new attempt while preserving prior attempts for audit and analytics. The backend uses NestJS with TypeORM and PostgreSQL. No internet connectivity, no external APIs, no OAuth, no email/SMS, no cloud storage. Security: salted password hashing, field-level encryption for sensitive notes, masking in audit exports, row-level access by role/store, immutable audit trails retained 7 years. Performance: p95 ≤ 300ms reads, ≤ 800ms writes at 50 req/s on single Docker host.
8	
9	---
10	
11	## 1. Mandatory Thresholds
12	
13	**1.1 Runability**
14	- Does `docker compose up` start cleanly with no errors?
15	- Does the app connect to PostgreSQL and run migrations on startup?
16	- Does `GET /health` return 200?
17	- Does `run_tests.sh` execute and produce a clear PASS/FAIL result?
18	- Does Swagger UI load at `http://localhost:3000/api/docs`?
19	
20	**1.2 Theme Alignment**
21	- Is this a pure backend API platform (no UI required)?
22	- Does it run fully offline with no external dependencies?
23	
24	---
25	
26	## 2. Delivery Completeness Checklist
27	
28	### Authentication and RBAC
29	- [ ] `POST /auth/login` — local username/password, returns JWT
30	- [ ] JWT middleware on all protected routes
31	- [ ] Role enum: platform_admin, store_admin, content_reviewer, auditor
32	- [ ] Store assignment on store_admin
33	- [ ] Wrong role → 403
34	- [ ] Audit log on login, logout, role change
35	- [ ] Account lockout after failed attempts
36	
37	### Resource Modeling
38	- [ ] CRUD: study rooms, zones, seats
39	- [ ] Seat attributes: power_outlet, quiet_zone, ada_accessible (booleans)
40	- [ ] Seat status: available, disabled, maintenance
41	- [ ] Seat map draft → publish flow
42	- [ ] Change note validation: 20–500 chars required on publish
43	- [ ] Immutable SeatMapVersion created on every publish
44	- [ ] Version history endpoint
45	
46	### Seat Reservations
47	- [ ] `POST /reservations` creates hold (holdUntil = now + 15 min)
48	- [ ] Maintenance seat → 400
49	- [ ] Already-held seat → 409
50	- [ ] `POST /reservations/:id/confirm` confirms hold
51	- [ ] `POST /reservations/:id/cancel` releases hold
52	- [ ] Background job releases expired holds every 60 seconds
53	- [ ] Expired hold confirm → 409
54	
55	### Commerce: Products
56	- [ ] SPU CRUD, SKU CRUD (unique per SPU)
57	- [ ] Categories and brands CRUD
58	- [ ] Specification attributes per SKU (jsonb)
59	- [ ] Tiered/member pricing per SKU
60	- [ ] Publish/unpublish with Content Reviewer approval workflow
61	- [ ] Store-scoped access for store_admin (cross-store → 403)
62	- [ ] Low-stock alert at configurable threshold (default 10 units)
63	
64	### Commerce: Inventory
65	- [ ] InventoryLot CRUD (batch code, expiration date, quantity)
66	- [ ] Stock adjustment requires reason code
67	- [ ] Idempotency key required on adjustments
68	- [ ] Duplicate idempotency key returns original response
69	- [ ] Expiration date alert notifications
70	
71	### Commerce: Orders
72	- [ ] `POST /orders` requires idempotency key
73	- [ ] State machine: pending → confirmed → fulfilled → cancelled
74	- [ ] Cancellation only from pending/confirmed → else 409
75	- [ ] Duplicate idempotency key returns original order (200 not 201)
76	- [ ] Store-scoped order history
77	
78	### Marketing Rules
79	- [ ] Coupon CRUD (code unique, validity window, remaining quantity)
80	- [ ] Claim, distribute, redeem, expire endpoints
81	- [ ] Threshold discount promotions
82	- [ ] Percentage discount promotions
83	- [ ] First-order offer detection
84	- [ ] Campaign time windows and per-campaign redemption caps
85	- [ ] Conflict resolution: priority (1–1000) then best customer value
86	- [ ] Deterministic tie-breaker (lower UUID if discount amounts equal)
87	- [ ] Max one coupon + one automatic promotion per order
88	
89	### Practice Assessment: Questions
90	- [ ] Question CRUD (objective/subjective)
91	- [ ] Answer options for objective questions
92	- [ ] Explanation/answer versioning
93	- [ ] Bulk import (JSON)
94	- [ ] Bulk export (CSV)
95	- [ ] Wrong-answer aggregation stats per question
96	- [ ] Content Reviewer approval workflow
97	
98	### Practice Assessment: Papers and Attempts
99	- [ ] Paper generation: random (count-based)
100	- [ ] Paper generation: rule-based (filter-based)
101	- [ ] Attempt create and submit
102	- [ ] Auto-grading for objective questions
103	- [ ] Score calculation and gradedAt timestamp
104	- [ ] Practice history per user
105	- [ ] Redo: new attempt from same paper, parent_attempt_id set
106	- [ ] Prior attempts preserved on redo
107	
108	### Data Quality and Observability
109	- [ ] Data quality rules CRUD (completeness, range, uniqueness per entity type)
110	- [ ] Quality score 0–100 computed per entity type
111	- [ ] On-demand quality score computation endpoint
112	- [ ] Scheduled quality score recomputation (every hour)
113	- [ ] Freshness monitoring (24-hour staleness threshold)
114	- [ ] Persisted notifications for admins
115	- [ ] Scheduled freshness check (every hour)
116	
117	### Security
118	- [ ] bcrypt password hashing (rounds=12)
119	- [ ] AES-256-GCM field-level encryption for sensitive notes
120	- [ ] Masking in audit log exports (`[REDACTED]` for sensitive fields)
121	- [ ] Row-level access: store_admin filtered by store_id on all queries
122	- [ ] Audit log append-only (no DELETE for app DB role)
123	- [ ] 7-year retention enforced at DB level
124	- [ ] Trace ID on every request (X-Trace-Id header + logs)
125	
126	---
127	
128	## 3. Engineering Quality Checklist
129	
130	### Module Structure (verify each exists)
131	- [ ] `src/auth/` — login, JWT, password, guards
132	- [ ] `src/rooms/` — rooms, zones, seats, versioning
133	- [ ] `src/reservations/` — holds, confirm, cancel, expiry job
134	- [ ] `src/products/` — SPU, SKU, categories, brands
135	- [ ] `src/inventory/` — lots, adjustments, alerts
136	- [ ] `src/orders/` — state machine, idempotency
137	- [ ] `src/promotions/` — coupons, discounts, conflict resolution
138	- [ ] `src/questions/` — question bank, versioning, bulk ops
139	- [ ] `src/assessments/` — papers, attempts, grading
140	- [ ] `src/quality/` — rules, scores, freshness
141	- [ ] `src/notifications/` — persisted notifications
142	- [ ] `src/audit/` — append-only audit log
143	- [ ] `src/common/` — guards, interceptors, filters, pipes
144	- [ ] `migrations/` — TypeORM migration files
145	- [ ] `unit_tests/` and `API_tests/` at repo root
146	- [ ] `run_tests.sh` at repo root
147	- [ ] `docker-compose.yml`, `Dockerfile`, `.env.example`
148	- [ ] `README.md` with startup command, ports, test credentials
149	
150	### Engineering Details
151	- [ ] Structured JSON logging (Winston) — not console.log
152	- [ ] Trace ID on every request
153	- [ ] class-validator on all DTOs
154	- [ ] Global exception filter returns `{statusCode, code, message, traceId}`
155	- [ ] TypeORM migrations (not sync:true in production)
156	- [ ] Config loaded from env (not hardcoded)
157	- [ ] Sensitive values never logged
158	- [ ] Swagger annotations on every controller
159	
160	---
161	
162	## 4. Security Audit Checklist (Priority)
163	
164	- [ ] `POST /auth/login` — bcrypt verify, lockout enforced
165	- [ ] JWT guard on every protected route
166	- [ ] Role guard enforced per endpoint
167	- [ ] store_admin: all queries filtered by store_id — cross-store returns 403
168	- [ ] Platform Admin can access all stores
169	- [ ] Content Reviewer: can only approve/reject products and questions
170	- [ ] Auditor: read-only on audit logs and dashboards — no write access
171	- [ ] Audit log: no DELETE endpoint exists, app DB role has INSERT only
172	- [ ] Sensitive fields in audit export masked as `[REDACTED]`
173	- [ ] Idempotency key prevents duplicate order creation
174	- [ ] Maintenance seat cannot be held (400, not 409)
175	- [ ] Expired hold confirm returns 409 (not 200)
176	- [ ] Promotion conflict resolution is deterministic (same input = same output always)
177	
178	---
179	
180	## 5. API Endpoint Completeness Checklist
181	
182	### Auth
183	- [ ] POST /auth/login
184	- [ ] GET /auth/me
185	- [ ] PATCH /auth/change-password
186	- [ ] GET /health
187	
188	### Users
189	- [ ] GET /users
190	- [ ] POST /users
191	- [ ] PATCH /users/:id
192	- [ ] DELETE /users/:id
193	
194	### Rooms
195	- [ ] GET/POST /rooms
196	- [ ] GET/PATCH/DELETE /rooms/:id
197	- [ ] GET/POST /rooms/:id/zones
198	- [ ] GET/POST /zones/:id/seats
199	- [ ] PATCH/DELETE /seats/:id
200	- [ ] POST /rooms/:id/publish
201	- [ ] GET /rooms/:id/versions
202	
203	### Reservations
204	- [ ] POST /reservations
205	- [ ] POST /reservations/:id/confirm
206	- [ ] POST /reservations/:id/cancel
207	- [ ] GET /reservations
208	
209	### Products
210	- [ ] GET/POST /products
211	- [ ] GET/PATCH/DELETE /products/:id
212	- [ ] GET/POST /products/:id/skus
213	- [ ] PATCH/DELETE /skus/:id
214	- [ ] GET/POST /categories
215	- [ ] GET/POST /brands
216	- [ ] POST /products/:id/publish
217	- [ ] POST /products/:id/unpublish
218	
219	### Inventory
220	- [ ] GET/POST /inventory/lots
221	- [ ] PATCH /inventory/lots/:id
222	- [ ] POST /inventory/adjust
223	
224	### Orders
225	- [ ] GET/POST /orders
226	- [ ] GET /orders/:id
227	- [ ] POST /orders/:id/confirm
228	- [ ] POST /orders/:id/cancel
229	- [ ] POST /orders/:id/fulfill
230	
231	### Promotions
232	- [ ] GET/POST /promotions
233	- [ ] PATCH/DELETE /promotions/:id
234	- [ ] GET/POST /coupons
235	- [ ] POST /coupons/:code/claim
236	- [ ] POST /coupons/:code/redeem
237	- [ ] POST /coupons/:id/expire
238	
239	### Questions
240	- [ ] GET/POST /questions
241	- [ ] GET/PATCH/DELETE /questions/:id
242	- [ ] POST /questions/:id/approve
243	- [ ] POST /questions/:id/reject
244	- [ ] POST /questions/import
245	- [ ] GET /questions/export
246	- [ ] GET /questions/:id/wrong-answer-stats
247	- [ ] GET/POST /questions/:id/explanations
248	
249	### Assessments
250	- [ ] GET/POST /papers
251	- [ ] GET /papers/:id
252	- [ ] POST /attempts

254	- [ ] POST /attempts/:id/redo
255	- [ ] GET /attempts/history
256	
257	### Quality
258	- [ ] GET/POST /quality/rules

261	
262	### Notifications
